tagline: Phoenix-based 24/7 semi truck and trailer repair, mobile service, DOT inspections, and emergency roadside.
primaryAudience: Long-haul truck drivers, owner-operators, and fleet managers in Phoenix, AZ and the Southwest corridor (I-10/I-17).
secondaryAudience: Trucking companies evaluating maintenance partners; brokers routing freight through Phoenix.

voice.tone: Practical, service-oriented, plain-language. Talks to drivers like a fellow shop pro who has been under the truck.
voice.personality: Solution-focused. Names the symptom, walks through the fix, ends with a clear way to get help.
voice.perspective: Written from the shop's POV — a Phoenix-based truck mechanic team that fixes thousands of issues a year.

doSay: Specific symptoms and diagnostic steps ("if air pressure drops below 80 psi at idle, check the governor first")
Mention Phoenix / Arizona / I-10 / I-17 naturally where geographically relevant
Service-page internal links woven into the body
Compliance and safety angle on DOT inspections, brakes, lights, ELD
Honest cost ranges with the variables that drive them
Pre-trip checks drivers can do themselves
Subtle CT Shop framing — "a shop that answers the phone 24/7"
dontSay: Buckle up / let's dive in / let's explore
AI tells: "In conclusion", "It's important to note", "At the end of the day"
groundingRules: CRITICAL: Use ONLY facts from siteFacts below or entities. Do NOT invent: specific cost ranges not listed, fleet sizes, certification claims, partnerships, or service offerings not in the list.
Use the EXACT phone number (602) 830-3232 in the closing CTA — do not substitute or modify.
When referencing services, use the EXACT names from siteFacts.services — these match the service-page URLs.
If a topic requires information not in siteFacts (e.g., specific certifications, awards, years in business), speak in industry generalities or omit that detail.

When citing facts that aren't about the company itself (fuel prices, regulations, industry trends), link to one of the approved externalLinks domains as the source. Do NOT invent statistics or cite unnamed 'experts/studies'.

entities.serviceArea: Phoenix, Avondale, Buckeye, Chandler, Gilbert, Glendale, Goodyear, Mesa, Peoria, Scottsdale

siteFacts.availability: 24/7 emergency truck and trailer repair + mobile service in Phoenix, AZ and surrounding metro.
siteFacts.services: Emergency truck repair (24/7), mobile repair on-site, semi truck AC repair, tire and brake repair, electrical diagnostics, engine repair, trailer suspension, full-body trailer repair, commercial fleet repair contracts, DOT inspections, breakdown towing/recovery.
siteFacts.serviceArea: Phoenix metro and surrounding Arizona cities: Avondale, Buckeye, Chandler, Gilbert, Glendale, Goodyear, Mesa, Peoria, Scottsdale. I-10 and I-17 corridor breakdowns covered.
siteFacts.specialty: Heavy-duty and semi-truck repair. Diesel mechanics with experience across Cummins, Detroit, Cat, Volvo, Mack, International, Freightliner, Peterbilt, Kenworth.
siteFacts.fleetContracts: Commercial fleet repair contracts available — scheduled PM service, fleet account management, priority booking.
siteFacts.dotCompliance: DOT annual inspections in Phoenix, AZ. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) compliance support.
siteFacts.emergencyResponse: Wrecker / towing service plus on-site mobile repair. Available 24 hours.

general.q: What services does CT Shop offer?
general.a: Heavy-duty truck and trailer repair, emergency roadside service, mobile/on-site repair, semi truck AC repair, brake and tire service, engine repair, trailer electrical, suspension, DOT inspections, and fleet maintenance contracts.

general.q: Do you service trucks 24/7 in Phoenix?
general.a: Yes — CT Shop runs 24/7 emergency truck repair and roadside service across Phoenix and surrounding cities.
Phoenix metro and surrounding Arizona cities including Avondale, Buckeye, Chandler, Gilbert, Glendale, Goodyear, Mesa, Peoria, and Scottsdale. I-10 and I-17 corridor breakdowns covered.

structureRules.format: HTML body (rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML by BlogPost.tsx)
structureRules.openingPattern: Start with a 2-sentence TL;DR summary that captures the main takeaway, then a hook addressing the reader's frustration or question.
structureRules.ctaPlacement: End with a CTA paragraph mentioning the phone number (602) 830-3232 + relevant service-page link
structureRules.images: Hero image picked from blogCoverImages (workflow picks one). Optional 1-2 in-body via
structureRules.externalLinkCount: 1-2 woven into sentences, only from approved externalLinks list

_note: siteFacts + siteFAQs are derived from the actual cttruckshop.com page content and source files. The LLM uses these for company-specific claims and must NOT invent operational specifics. Update when CT Shop's services, phone, or service area change.
